MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar May-20-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. PAMELA E. GLAZNER, Esq. of Emanuel Law Group (Tel: 650-369-8900) for plaintiff;
Atty. JAMAL J. ATIBA, Esq. of Edlin Gallagher Huie + Blum (Tel: 415-397-9006) for specially appearing defendant Baryalai Zazai.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT BARYALAI ZAZAI'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification, as follows:

Defendant BARYALAI ZAZAI's motion to quash is denied. Plaintiff meets his burden of demonstrating substitute service in compliance with CCP 415.20(b). (See Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1439-1440 [plaintiff's burden to demonstrate jurisdiction including proper service].)

The Oakland residence where the substitute service occurred is defendant's "dwelling house, usual place of abode, or usual mailing address." (CCP 415.20(b).) Defendant declares that he is currently living in Afghanistan and he does not know when, if at all, he will be able to return to the United States of America. (Zazai Decl., 8.) Defendant does not expressly state that he no longer considers the Oakland apartment his residence and his prior declaration shows that it is his current abode and remained so even after he went to Afghanistan. (Emanuel Decl., Ex 4, at 7 ["I was not at my residence at 3525 Maybelle Avenue, Apartment G, Oakland, CA 94619, on December 16, 2021."].)

Plaintiff demonstrates due diligence with the multiple attempts to serve defendant on March 16, 2022, and previously. Plaintiff further shows that he served defendant's roommate, the roommate was informed of the contents of the served papers, and plaintiff mailed a copy of the summons and complaint to defendant. Plaintiff therefore effectuated substitute service and the court notes that the service statutes are liberally construed where there is actual notice. (See Pasadena Medi-Center Associates v.Superior Court (1973) 9 Cal.3d 773, 778.)

Defendant has 30 days from today to respond.

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/SRB)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Pamela E. Glazner, Esq., EMANUEL LAW GROUP, (650) 369-8900, for Plaintiff ALESSANDRO ISOLANI,
Jamal J. Atiba, Esq., EDLIN GALLAGHER HUIE + BLUM, (415) 397-9006, jatiba@eghblaw.com for Specially Appearing Defendant BARYALAI ZAZAI
Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

DEFENDANT BARYALAI ZAZAI MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

Baryalai Zazai's "motion to quash service of summons and complaint" is granted. When a defendant challenges personal jurisdiction, the burden is on plaintiff to prove effective service. (Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1439-40.)

A process server claims she personally served Zazai in Oakland on December 16, 2021, but Zazai declares under penalty of perjury that he was in Afghanistan at the time, which is supported by his airline tickets and passport. (Zazai Decl. 4-8.)

Plaintiff alternatively now claims "substituted service" - apparently on a roommate - under CCP 415.20(b). (Opp. 2:20-3:20.) However, both the process server's declaration and the "proof of service summons" attached to it assert "personal service," not "substituted service." Moreover, the process server concedes that, when the roommate "asked me what they [the "legal documents"] were about I replied that I did not know but that he needed to read them." This was not "inform[ing] of the contents thereof" as CCP 415.20(b) requires.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
